master_row.title: Metric Degeneracy Condition
master_row.canonical_statement: At the TZP, the metric tensor satisfies $\det g|_p = 0$ and the inverse metric $g^{-1}|_p$ does not exist; for $x\neq p$, $\det g(x)\neq 0$ and $g^{-1}(x)$ exists.
master_row.canonical_equation: \det g\vert_{p}=0 || g^{-1}\vert_{p}\ \text{does not exist}, \qquad \det g(x)\neq 0 \ \text{for}\ x\neq p
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as an algebraic definition relating g, p, x.
master_row.dictionary: At the TZP, the metric tensor satisfies ( g_p=0) and (g^-1_p) does not exist; away from (p), ( g 0).
master_row.encyclopedia: ID 0004 strengthens ID 0001 by explicitly separating the ordinary region from the TZP degeneracy region. Interpretive role: This condition permits singular curvature behavior without abandoning ordinary geometry away from the anchor. It becomes the gateway for time dilation, field nonlinearity, and generalized measures defined only after normalization.
